top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer's defining structure — sat / asat as 'has being / has no being', tattva-darśin named verbatim as the operational role, the seers seeing the conclusion of both — anchors specifically on BG 2.16's grammar. The phantom-bug-vs-real-need pairing matches the verse's two-pronged diagnosis (no being / no non-being).

Distant secondary candidate: 18.61 'Iśvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ' speaks of the Lord situated in the heart governing all beings — vaguely connected to 'real signal' framing — but the analog here is not about a controlling agent, it is about ontological discrimination.
BG 13.27 ('seeing the same in all') has a 'seer' theme — but the seeing is of equality across beings, not of the sat/asat cut. Distant.
